programme.desc: Rob and Gerald brave the red carpet at a celebrity hotspot in London to try to recover the unpaid wages of a former worker, and there is an angry confrontation in a motorcycle shop when Mike and Lawrence try to remove bikes to collect a debt owed to a customer(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and Nick Hall help two teams look for items in the West Sussex town of Ardingly, while presenter Anita Manning investigates the history of funfairs(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Eve is a little more optimistic about the future and Valerie faces a tragedy, while Rob sustains an injury at work, and one of his colleagues acts strangely around the hospital(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in rural Somerset with two sport enthusiasts who have a £1million budget and want to buy a country home with views and a garden for football(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell go in search for items in Cumbria, setting out from Cockermouth and heading towards an auction near Kendal(n)

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by three of the couples, including the first to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Matt Baker and Michelle Ackerley present(n)

programme.title: Inside Out South
programme.desc: A report on a plan to save the seaweed forests off the Sussex coast that has the backing of David Attenborough and which could help combat climate change. Plus, the programme goes on the trail of a rogue travel agent who took thousands of pounds from families to pay for holidays that were never booked, leaving them to pay twice or go without(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben agrees to go on a date with Callum, although he begins to wonder if it is such a good idea after both Ian and Jay comment on his history with men - and things only get worse when Stuart pleads with him not to hurt his brother. Bex is fed up of Sonia fussing over university so by way of distraction she organises a baby shower for Louise. Mel continues to pressurise Sharon into getting her a villa in Portugal, Denise confronts Wanda over her treatment of Ted and Jean supports Daniel at his hospital appointments(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A year on from the assassination of Jamal Khashoggi, Jane Corbin investigates the brutal murder of the journalist inside the Saudi consulate in Istanbul. She speaks to some of the very few people who have listened to covert recordings from inside the diplomatic building, revealing the full details of what happened to Khashoggi, the hit squad sent to kill him and the cover-up that followed. She also hears evidence that responsibility for the murder goes to the highest levels of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and asks whether the West has turned a blind eye to state-sanctioned murder so that lucrative trade deals can flourish(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The eight remaining contestants take part in the first of the semi-finals and face their toughest challenge yet as they battle gale force winds as they cook in tents perched high on top of the White Cliffs of Dover. They need to defy the elements to serve up a feast for 120 HM Coastguard staff and volunteers responsible for keeping one of the world's busiest shipping lanes safe. They then head back to the MasterChef kitchen for an invention test, before two more celebrities are sent home(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a documentary following young people from two local estates in London's Waltham Forest as they take part in a ground-breaking arts intervention programme designed to change the course of their lives for good. With help from mentors including Young Vic artistic director Kwame Kwei-Armah, EastEnders star Tameka Empson and composer Talvin Singh, the cast write and perform a musical based on their lives, as part of the capital's first Borough of Culture celebrations(n)

programme.title: The Cameron Years
programme.desc: The second of the two-part documentary begins in 2005 at a moment of crisis for the Conservative Party, following three successive general election defeats. David Cameron, a relatively new MP, and his small group of supporters sought to radically shift the party's direction, taking on veteran opponent David Davis in a leadership election. Here, Cameron re-examines his desire to reshape the party - and how he risked alienating the powerful old guard as he set out to prioritise issues such as environmentalism, social justice and international aid(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela Rippon test drives an electric car and discovers if the network of charging points needed is enough to support the vehicles. Plus, the dangers of online banking(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin check out a chalet bungalow in Beccles, Norfolk, and projects in Cwmaman in Wales and Sneyd Green, near Stoke(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: The cases of a lodger who killed his landlord and fled to Ireland, a homeless man with two years to serve on a prison sentence, and a Polish drink driver on the run in London(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Two sheriffs are forced to call the police when they are confronted by a gang of threatening men, while another is in for a surprise during a call to a debtor's house(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from London's Portobello Road, as experts Natasha Raskin and Thomas Plant support the red and blue teams scouring the famous street market. Catherine Southon acts as auctioneer(n)

programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a garlic specialist who wants to establish a new business to find a new home in Shropshire on a £550 000 budget. Plus, a look at the expert craft of harp-making(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steph Connell has a birthday surprise for Phil Serrell as they travel from Dumfries and Galloway to Edinburgh, stopping to look for gold in the highest village in the country(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal talks to the couple who managed to avoid elimination at the weekend, and takes a closer look at the performances. Plus, interviews with the remaining dancers(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Matt Baker and Michelle Ackerley present(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Stuart is secretly pleased that Ben and Callum's date did not go to plan, although his mood switches when the Highway brothers receives a surprise visit from dad Jonno. Bex continues to struggle at the thought of leaving Walford and reminisces about Shakil. Jack feels out of his depth at his first day back with the police, Denise's obsession with Sheree and Patrick proves to be the final straw and Jean tries to keep Daniel's spirits up(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Chloe is interviewed over her assault, while Ange battles to keep Evan alive, but with so many conflicting versions of events, it remains uncertain if the truth will out. Cameron is furious, and determined to go to any lengths to see justice is done. Ric tries to support his granddaughter while secretly arranging an MRI scan to uncover the cause of his declining cognitive abilities(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: The truth behind what happened to Shaun is revealed to him as he finally comes face to face with the powers behind his downfall. Rachel seeks answers of her own after her suspicions that Shaun has been set up are confirmed by a vital piece of evidence. However, the truth they both uncover places their lives in great danger(n)

programme.title: Junior Doctors: On the Front Line
programme.desc: The doctors spend their first Christmas working in the hospital. Zohaib faces a difficult conversation, Nick deals with an awkward patient, and Howra has to certify her first death(n)

programme.title: Japan with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue Perkins continues on her trip around Japan, exploring a nation caught between the demands of a hi-tech future and the pull of a traditional past. In the concluding episode, Sue travels to the ancient city of Kyoto to spend time with geishas and meets those working hard to keep geisha culture alive. She then heads south of Kyoto to experience free diving with Ama divers, before travelling to Hiroshima, where she meets Mr Tetsushi, an 84-year-old survivor of the atomic blast during the Second World War. Finally, Sue returns to Tokyo, to see how the Japanese are tackling challenges of the future(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Consumer affairs show tackling issues around the UK. In a battle against suspect deals, the team digs into the facts and demands answers from people at the top, as well as showing viewers how to get the necessary information to make practical choices about their finances(n)

programme.title: Doctors

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents further coverage of day six of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar, featuring the women's 200m and men's 110m hurdles. Dina Asher-Smith entered the 200m as one of the favourites to prevail in a discipline where there had not been a British medallist since Kathy Smallwood-Cook took bronze in the inaugural 1983 event in Helsinki. With analysis from Michael Johnson(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The business-based reality show returns. A new group of 16 hopeful candidates embark on their first challenge of the competition. The contestants are sent 6,000 miles from London to the capital city of Cape Town in South Africa, where they are tasked to set up and run their own safari and vineyard tours. One team's brash approach to ticket sales causes commotion, while on the other team premium prices prove problematic(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: Adam Frisby and the In The Style team get to work on a collection for 21-year-old Lottie Tomlinson, a social media influencer with over three million followers. Hoping to impress her, Adam decides to send her on a trip to Ibiza to shoot the campaign. While Lottie is ultimately happy with the items, Adam is concerned the designs are very niche and may not prove as popular as their previous collection(n)

programme.title: Stupid Man, Smart Phone
programme.desc: Russell Kane travels through Norway, heading into the Arctic Circle in the hope of seeing the Northern Lights. He is accompanied on his journey by YouTube stars Rose and Rosie, and together they rely on a smartphone to provide answers to all the challenges the expedition throws their way(n)

programme.desc: Arusha Irvine enjoys the fun of the fair, while Angus Ashworth heads to Plymouth to discover the turbulent history of gin, before having a go as a Master Distiller. They then join up at their final auction in Clevedon, north Somerset(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich are tasked to create a garden that fuses Japan and Yorkshire - in Cheshunt, Hertfordshire. Yorkshire-born James wants to see hints of his native county in his new Home Counties house, while his Japanese wife Akika, who he met when traveling, naturally wants to feel a link to her upbringing and homeland(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A lifeboat crew in Dorset faces the challenge of getting urgent medical aid to a rock climber, who has fallen more than 40 feet and landed on an inaccessible rocky ledge. In Cornwall, a paraglider misjudges his launch and ends up crashing into the sea, while the Redcar crew search for a man who has drifted over a mile offshore in a small toy dinghy(n)

programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

Great Britain's Dina Asher-Smith begins her bid for the 200m title in the heats, and the women's 400m also gets under way, while the women's javelin qualifying takes place in the field(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents further coverage of day four of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Norwegian star Karsten Warholm will be looking to defend his 400m hurdles title, but is expected to face competition from home favourite Abderrahman Samba and America's Rai Benjamin. Elsewhere, finals take place in the women's high jump, 800m and 3,000m steeplechase, and the men's 5,000m and discus(n)

programme.title: What Britain Buys and Sells in a Day
programme.desc: As the nation's trading relationships face their biggest change in decades, Ed Balls, Ade Adepitan and Cherry Healey explore the science and systems that enable Britain to import and export goods around the world on an unprecedented scale. Ed is at the London Gateway port to reveal all the tricks of the fruit and vegetable trade, Ade visits Peru to see how Britain's obsession with healthy eating has created a booming economy for avocado growers, and Cherry meets a British farmer keeping the Middle East supplied with apples(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Eurovision winner Dana International introduces a look back at a year in which Lock, Stock and Two Smoking Barrels shot director Guy Ritchie to fame, There's Something About Mary had cinema audiences roaring with laughter and adult animation South Park took the world by storm. Goodness Gracious Me received a warm reception, the campaign to free Coronation Street's Deirdre Rachid began and George Michael found himself in a compromising situation(n)

programme.title: Newsnight

programme.title: Today at Conference

programme.title: The Cult Next Door
programme.desc: The extraordinary story of a strange cult that came to light in 2013, when three women emerged from a small flat in Brixton, south London, after decades in captivity. The documentary traces the group back to its roots in the 1970s, when Aravindan Balakrishnan, who believed in an international communist revolution, created a tiny political sect that followed the teachings of China's Chairman Mao. However, his left-wing collective evolved into a pseudo-religious cult, in which members were controlled, threatened and brainwashed so that they were too terrified to leave(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The four remaining interior designers take part in a commercial design challenge to transform two restaurants in Bristol, as they look to claim their place in the semi-finals. For the first time, Michelle Ogundehin tells them they will have the chance to meet their clients and see the spaces before taking on the design, but also warns them that she needs to see some individual design flair from each of them, as at the end of the challenge, one designer from the losing team will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The programme plays host to One Man and His Dog 2019, as the best shepherds from across the British Isles descend on the Scottish Borders to find out which nation has what it takes to claim this coveted title. Matt Baker and Helen Skelton present proceedings from Bowhill House, as the best youngsters and senior shepherds from England, Wales, Ireland and Scotland are teamed up in a bid to win the trophy for their country(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell go in search for items in Cumbria, setting out from Cockermouth and heading towards an auction near Kendal(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: The work of police extradition units in the UK and Europe, following officers seeking out people fleeing from justice. Detectives in London search the capital for fugitives on the run from justice, and officers from Derby travel to Latvia to arrest a gang of people traffickers(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles hits the road to catch up with some of the inspirational people and communities the show has helped in its 20-year history. On his travels across the length and breadth of the country, Nick returns to some of the series' biggest and most challenging builds, including Canada Street in Manchester, The Yard in Edinburgh, and DIY SOS's most logistically-difficult and longest build - the construction of the Grenfell Community Centre and Dale Youth Boxing club(n)

programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of day five of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Home favourite Mutaz Essa Barshim is sure to get a warm welcome when he begins his defence of the high jump title in qualifying, while heats in the men's 400m and 3,000m steeplechase and women's 400m hurdles take place on the track(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents further coverage of day five of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. America's Noah Lyles is widely regarded as the man to beat in this evening's 200m final, having become the fourth fastest man in history this year when running a time of 19.50 in Lausanne, while Britons Adam Gemili and Zharnel Hughes will hope to have qualified. Elsewhere, Great Britain's Dina Asher-Smith continues her quest for titles in the 200m semi-finals, and medals are decided in the men's pole vault and 800m, and the women's javelin(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Sheepdog trainer Katy invites three potential partners to experience a taste of country life in the Cumbrian Fells. While the weather is bleak, painter and decorator John, motorbike champion David and former footballer Carl take to herding sheep and mucking out kennels with remarkable success. Grace takes a day off from her family's dairy to visit a hobby farmer on his own turf, while Sara Cox pays a visit to Cumbrian farmer David to deliver letters from six candidates for a speed dating session(n)

programme.title: Defending the Guilty
programme.desc: Caroline and Will take on a prosecution case at the last minute, but victory is made seemingly impossible by the combination of an underfunded system and a nervous elderly victim who just wants the whole thing over and done with. The competition between the pupils continues to heat up, as Liam and Danielle find incriminating evidence against Pia. Will feels he has been dishonest enough and wants no part in the dirty tricks being played to get ahead - but he soon finds that in this career, no one can afford to be an idealist(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories with Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Today at Conference
programme.desc: Jo Coburn reviews Tuesday's events at the Conservative Party's annual conference in Manchester. First shown on BBC Two on Tuesday 1 October.(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Jason Bell and two-time Super Bowl winner Osi Umenyiora preview all the big games from Week 4 of the 2019 regular season, including action from the Aaron Rodgers led Green Bay Packers against the Carson Wentz led Philadelphia Eagles(n)

programme.title: Inside the Vatican
programme.desc: The second of two programmes in which cameras record the inner workings of the Vatican over the course of a year. One of the most important tools of reform the Pope possesses is his power to appoint new cardinals. It's the closest he gets to succession planning, because the cardinals will elect the new Pope at the next conclave. Here, Pope Francis selects 14 new cardinals, breaking with tradition by choosing men outside the centres of power. As the pontiff prepares to make a first papal visit to Ireland, the Church is rocked by a report alleging the cover-up of sex abuses by priests, dating back decades(n)

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: It is 1933 and Adolf Hitler is the chancellor of Germany, but he does not have absolute power as there is still a democratic parliament, a head of state and the rule of law. Hitler calls a general election to increase Nazi representation, but a fire in the Reichstag building is blamed on communists - prompting thousands of arrests of left-wingers. Hitler then calls on President von Hindenburg to act and a decree is passed giving the Nazis emergency powers to ban free speech and the right to protest(n)

programme.title: One Day That Changed My Life
programme.desc: A man with muscular dystrophy takes his driving test in an attempt to gain independence and the villagers of Cheavely in Cambridgeshire try to save their church(n)

programme.title: Britain's Secret Charity Cheats
programme.desc: The fundraiser who faked cancer to trick his own family out of thousands, and the so-called 'ghost learners' used to scam an educational charity out of nearly £500,000(n)

This documentary follows her over the course of a year, revealing what the life of a social media influencer is really like(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of day six of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. The best all-round athletes in the world are in action today, as the decathlon and heptathlon get under way, with Great Britain's Katarina Johnson-Thompson expected to battle Belgium's Nafi Thiam for the title in the latter. With analysis from Jessica Ennis-Hill and Denise Lewis(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents further coverage of day six of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. The heptathlon continues with one of Katarina Johnson-Thompson's strongest events in the 200m, and the decathlon high jump also takes place. Plus, the semi-finals of the men's 110m hurdles and 400m and the women's 400m hurdles take place and the men's hammer final gets under way. With analysis from Jessica Ennis-Hill and Denise Lewis(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The two remaining designers compete in the final of the competition, and are challenged to redesign an entire open plan apartment in Manchester. Before the finalists start the design process, knowing they need to make it count, they head off to Manchester, to see the apartments for the first time. Meeting the homeowners, they realise they must design for the client, but also work hard to put their own design stamp on the properties. With just three days to complete this ambitious challenge, the finalists know they have their work cut out if they're to prove to Michelle that they are the worthy winner(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents concluding coverage of day six of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar, featuring the 400m discipline of the decathlon and reaction to the men's 110m hurdles final. With analysis from Michael Johnson(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice: You're Fired
programme.desc: The companion programme to The Apprentice returns, with Tom Allen talking to the first candidate to be ejected from the competition. The host is also joined by a panel of experts and fans, who offer an entertaining analysis of this week's task(n)

programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Today at Conference
programme.desc: Highlights from the Conservative Party autumn conference(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass examine the case of Edith Thompson, who was convicted of murdering her husband Percy and sentenced to death in 1922(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: James May: The Reassembler
programme.desc: James tries to put back together a 1959 Suffolk Colt petrol lawnmower, which helped make mowing accessible to the masses by producing a smaller and affordable machine(n)

programme.title: Cake Bakers and Trouble Makers
programme.desc: In a programme originally shown to mark the Women's Institute's centenary year, historian Lucy Worsley goes beyond the stereotypes of jam and Jerusalem to reveal the surprisingly radical side of this British institution. She reveals that the WI's humble origins in an Anglesey garden shed in 1915 were no bar to the movement's grand ambitions to be an organisation engaged in the fight for women's rights. She explores some of the WI's most important campaigns, including its 1918 crusade for decent housing and its remarkably radical fight for equal pay in 1943(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: On June 4, 1989 ,the world's biggest, longest, and most famous pro-democracy demonstration was brought to a tragic end in Tiananmen Square. The images from those final bloody days remain potent and the death toll is still disputed. This documentary combines eyewitness accounts and leaked secret documents to provide a deeper understanding of how the events of those seven weeks unfolded in Beijing and behind the scenes, changing the course of Chinese history.(n)

programme.title: I Was There: Kate Adie on Tiananmen Square
programme.desc: Kate Adie re-examines her historic coverage of the massacre in Tiananmen Square, Beijing, in June 1989, revealing how she was wounded by gunfire and narrowly escaped death herself as an estimated 2,000 pro-democracy demonstrators were shot by Chinese government troops. Drawing on the BBC film archive, she also assesses how China has been portrayed before and after this period of upheaval, with additional insight from historian Steve Tsang(n)

programme.title: The Story of China
programme.desc: Michael Wood tells the story of the world's newest superpower, exploring the stories, people, culture and landscapes that have helped create its character over 4,000 years. He begins by looking at ancestry, one of the great themes of Chinese history. He joins a 300-strong family as they reunite to carry out rituals for an ancestor who died in 1049, witnesses a million pilgrims at the shrine of the goddess Nu Wa, and hears about China's greatest archaeological discovery, the Oracle Bones, which reveal fascinating clues about the first great dynasty - the Shang(n)

programme.title: The Incredible Story of Marie Antoinette's Watch with Nicholas Parsons
programme.desc: The Just a Minute host explores the life and work of Swiss horologist Abraham-Louis Breguet and goes in search of his most famous timepiece, which is valued at up to $100m(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: A teenage girl is found dead, and Laure is convinced there is a link to the Mercier murder, but proof remains elusive. Meanwhile, Tintin's misbehaving son becomes a cause for concern, and Josephine cracks ahead of a crucial court appearance. French crime drama, starring Caroline Proust(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: The detectives continue their mission to track down the murdered teenager's best friend, and a sexual harassment hearing lays bare Josephine's feelings. Meanwhile, Roban and Laure discover evidence of police corruption. In French(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: James May: The Reassembler
programme.desc: The presenter continues in his quest to put history back together again one piece at a time by tackling a 1957 Bakelite dial telephone, a challenge involving 211 pieces(n)

programme.title: England's Forgotten Queen: The Life and Death of Lady Jane Grey
programme.desc: Helen Castor concludes the docudrama telling the story of the woman who reigned England and Ireland for nine days in 1553. The historian looks at the final days of her reign, when she had been installed in the Tower of London, only for Mary Tudor's forces to assemble ready for battle. And when one of Jane's key's supporters defected to Mary's side, taking thousands of supporters, the balance tipped and the men who installed her on the throne abandoned her(n)

programme.title: The Troubles: A Secret History
programme.desc: Jennifer O'Leary examines how British intelligence used informers to penetrate the IRA, revealing the one flaw in the organisation's supposedly impenetrable security that was used to unlock its secrets. The strategy culminated in the Loughgall ambush, a face-off between the SAS and the IRA that left eight members of the paramilitary group dead(n)

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Paul Rose investigates Norwegian explorer Fridtjof Nansen's expedition to reach the North Pole in 1893. Despite his failure to persuade any scientists to join him, Nansen became a pioneer of polar exploration techniques when he allowed his ship to become stuck in pack ice, hoping it would drift toward his destination(n)

programme.title: The Last Explorers
programme.desc: The story of John Muir, the precursor of conservationist campaigns and one of the founders of America's National Park movement. The adventurer devoted his life to exploring the natural world and worked to convince institutions and the general public of the importance of the environment. Neil Oliver presents(n)

programme.title: Ireland's Treasures Uncovered
programme.desc: Documentary about the Irish artefacts that have helped to shape and create modern Ireland, both north and south. Alice Roberts and Gavin Hughes uncover the tales behind treasures such as the Tara Brooch, the Broighter Hoard, the Waterford Charter Roll and others(n)

programme.title: The Art That Made Mexico: Paradise, Power and Prayers
programme.desc: Alinka Echeverria explores the three major forces that have shaped Mexican art, as well as the country itself, beginning by exploring the influence of nature and land(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Claiming vengeance has repercussions for Josephine, and the team's reputation takes a hit in the wake of the disastrous Roma camp raid. Seeking to redeem themselves, the detectives try to uncover evidence against the corrupt cops, and Laure must make some difficult decisions for baby Romy. French crime thriller, starring Caroline Proust and Audrey Fleurot(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Keeping an eye on a large pool of suspects puts a strain on the detectives' resources, but eventually yields results. Meanwhile, Josephine challenges Roban over the male escort case, and Gilou has a heart-to-heart with Laure. French crime thriller, starring Audret Fleurot(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: James May: The Reassembler
programme.desc: The presenter continues in his quest to understand everyday objects by putting them back together again, this time reassembling the 147 pieces of an electric guitar. James' task will entail soldering, extensive use of his precision Japanese screwdrivers, and some fiddly electronics, before he attempts to channel Brian May in a solo performance(n)

programme.title: Dig WW2 with Dan Snow
programme.desc: The historian travels to Lough Foyle to explore the history of a Flying Fortress that crashed while travelling from America to an air force base near Bedford. He unearths the stories of the crew's survival against the odds and learns how a local woman and her boat contributed to their rescue. He also visits the disused Cluntoe airfield in Co Tyrone, where RAF pilots were trained for flying missions all over Europe, and looks back at the climatic end to the Battle of the Atlantic on the River Foyle docks(n)

programme.title: A Day in the Life of Earth
programme.desc: Mathematician Hannah Fry examines how much the planet can change in just 24 hours and how these daily changes are essential to humanity's existence. She looks at how new land is created by lava from volcanoes, and that the Moon not only causes huge movements of water in the oceans in the form of tides, but also creates waves of solid rock on land(n)

programme.title: Rik Mayall: Lord of Misrule
programme.desc: Comedian and actor Rik Mayall died suddenly in June 2014, but left a body of work that spanned four decades and was a blend of rocket-fuelled physical comedy, surrealism, subversive satire and pompous punk wit. Mayall's characters included the Black Country investigative nerd Kevin Turvey, Felicity Kendal-adoring student poet Rick in The Young Ones, unemployed oddball Richard Richard in Bottom, ruthless MP Alan B'Stard in The New Statesman and the roguish Lord Flashheart, who appeared in the second and fourth series of Blackadder. Narrated by Simon Callow, this documentary celebrates Mayall's part in British comedy history, with contributions by Michael Palin, Simon Pegg, Lenny Henry, Ben Elton, Alexei Sayle, Christopher Ryan, Tim McInnerny, Jools Holland, Ruby Wax and Greg Davies(n)

programme.title: Stories from the Dark Earth: Meet the Ancestors Revisited
programme.desc: Archaeologist Julian Richards revisits some of his most important digs to discover how science, conservation and new finds have changed people's understanding of ancient history. His journey continues with two excavations that illustrate the vastly different social roles occupied by a pair of Iron Age women. The first site uncovers evidence of the possible ritual sacrifice of a teenage girl from the Cotswolds, while the second unearths the chariot burial of a queen from 400BC, whose well preserved possessions are leading to astonishing conclusions about belief in early times(n)

programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa follow a trail of muddy footprints and find Po in bed with very muddy feet. The Noo-noo cleans off the mud(n)

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with blocks and enjoy songs and story time together(n)

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: The little boy is fascinated with the way the zipper on his coat closes up and opens again. With his friends in the Art World, he explores the magic of opening and closing things(n)

programme.title: My First
programme.desc: Willow visits a funfair for the very first time. She builds up the courage to go on the waltzer and later wins a big monkey teddy on the games(n)

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day goes on another time-travelling adventure to collect barnacles from a prehistoric ocean for his new museum display. Along the way, he tracks down a giant prehistoric whale(n)

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Angelo, Alisha and Louie take on the Coconutty Raft and Tangled Net in pursuit of Gem's jewels, while Cook mistakes the chocolate powder for gunpowder in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The big dog has something interesting to show the squirrels, but it is a bit prickly, so they go off into the garden to find their own things to show and tell(n)

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: Can Team Tinpo bring a pair of separated friends back together?(n)

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: When all the bees disappear, Messy and friends meet bee royalty and save the day(n)

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie learns how digger tracks help it move and she finds out how wellies are made(n)

programme.title: Catie's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies rally driver Catie shows off three amazing building machines(n)

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: The rabbit loses Cotton-tail's beloved squeaky toy, and must defeat his enemies to get it back(n)

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world(n)

programme.title: Gigglebiz
programme.desc: DIY Dan builds a birdbath, and Dina Lady makes a sticky toffee pudding in the Bake Me a Star competition. Meanwhile, Keith Fitt takes part in a hurdle race(n)

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Animated fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family(n)

programme.title: Time for School
programme.desc: It is Remembrance Day at the school and the children make poppies to remember those who died in the wars(n)

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo learns about different types of work and decides to visit a busy construction site(n)

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Lambkin must catch the tub wen it rolls away with Nana inside it(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny and Small accidentally spill green soup all over Granny's best shawl, and when they ask Cloud and the froglets to help them clean up the mess, things only end up getting worse(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy dances in the garden and the Pinky Ponk copies her every move(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Kym Marsh reads How Many Sleeps?, by Amber Stewart and Layn Marlow(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Consumer affairs show tackling issues around the UK. In a battle against suspect deals, the team digs into the facts and demands answers from people at the top, as well as showing viewers how to get the necessary information to make practical choices about their finances(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin explore a two-bed terrace in Salford, a three-bed semi in Derbyshire and a one-bedroom flat in south London(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Following the investigation by an elite team of Scotland Yard detectives into the biggest organised crime gangs in London, which uncovered the drugs kingpin behind a multimillion-pound racket. By making his own drugs delivery, Paul Monk blew his cover and, when his associates were arrested, fled abroad(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Rob and Gerald brave the red carpet at a celebrity hotspot in London to try to recover the unpaid wages of a former worker, and there is an angry confrontation in a motorcycle shop when Mike and Lawrence try to remove bikes to collect a debt owed to a customer(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and Nick Hall help two teams look for items in the West Sussex town of Ardingly, while presenter Anita Manning investigates the history of funfairs(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Eve is a little more optimistic about the future and Valerie faces a tragedy, while Rob sustains an injury at work, and one of his colleagues acts strangely around the hospital(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in rural Somerset with two sport enthusiasts who have a £1million budget and want to buy a country home with views and a garden for football(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell go in search for items in Cumbria, setting out from Cockermouth and heading towards an auction near Kendal(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by three of the couples, including the first to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Matt Baker and Michelle Ackerley present(n)

programme.title: Down the Social with Owen Money
programme.desc: The comedian and Mandy Starr head to Neath Working Men's Social Club to discover the pulling power of tribute acts inspired by stars from Tina Turner to Tom Jones. They meet two sisters who have been coming to the club weekly for half a century, and Owen has a lesson in bingo etiquette(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben agrees to go on a date with Callum, although he begins to wonder if it is such a good idea after both Ian and Jay comment on his history with men - and things only get worse when Stuart pleads with him not to hurt his brother. Bex is fed up of Sonia fussing over university so by way of distraction she organises a baby shower for Louise. Mel continues to pressurise Sharon into getting her a villa in Portugal, Denise confronts Wanda over her treatment of Ted and Jean supports Daniel at his hospital appointments(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Investigates
programme.desc: Wyre Davies is on the trail of the people behind the multimillion-pound puppy industry to find out what is really going on inside licensed premises. In this BBC Wales Investigates programme, he confronts the breeders, inspectors and vets who should be policing the trade(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The eight remaining contestants take part in the first of the semi-finals and face their toughest challenge yet as they battle gale force winds as they cook in tents perched high on top of the White Cliffs of Dover. They need to defy the elements to serve up a feast for 120 HM Coastguard staff and volunteers responsible for keeping one of the world's busiest shipping lanes safe. They then head back to the MasterChef kitchen for an invention test, before two more celebrities are sent home(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A year on from the assassination of Jamal Khashoggi, Jane Corbin investigates the brutal murder of the journalist inside the Saudi consulate in Istanbul. She speaks to some of the very few people who have listened to covert recordings from inside the diplomatic building, revealing the full details of what happened to Khashoggi, the hit squad sent to kill him and the cover-up that followed. She also hears evidence that responsibility for the murder goes to the highest levels of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and asks whether the West has turned a blind eye to state-sanctioned murder so that lucrative trade deals can flourish(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a documentary following young people from two local estates in London's Waltham Forest as they take part in a ground-breaking arts intervention programme designed to change the course of their lives for good. With help from mentors including Young Vic artistic director Kwame Kwei-Armah, EastEnders star Tameka Empson and composer Talvin Singh, the cast write and perform a musical based on their lives, as part of the capital's first Borough of Culture celebrations(n)

programme.title: The Cameron Years
programme.desc: The second of the two-part documentary begins in 2005 at a moment of crisis for the Conservative Party, following three successive general election defeats. David Cameron, a relatively new MP, and his small group of supporters sought to radically shift the party's direction, taking on veteran opponent David Davis in a leadership election. Here, Cameron re-examines his desire to reshape the party - and how he risked alienating the powerful old guard as he set out to prioritise issues such as environmentalism, social justice and international aid(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela Rippon test drives an electric car and discovers if the network of charging points needed is enough to support the vehicles. Plus, the dangers of online banking(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin check out a chalet bungalow in Beccles, Norfolk, and projects in Cwmaman in Wales and Sneyd Green, near Stoke(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: The cases of a lodger who killed his landlord and fled to Ireland, a homeless man with two years to serve on a prison sentence, and a Polish drink driver on the run in London(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Two sheriffs are forced to call the police when they are confronted by a gang of threatening men, while another is in for a surprise during a call to a debtor's house(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from London's Portobello Road, as experts Natasha Raskin and Thomas Plant support the red and blue teams scouring the famous street market. Catherine Southon acts as auctioneer(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie's tragic news divides opinion at The Mill, Zara tries to hide her hatred of Becky, and Rob faces outrage from a victim support group(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a garlic specialist who wants to establish a new business to find a new home in Shropshire on a £550 000 budget. Plus, a look at the expert craft of harp-making(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steph Connell has a birthday surprise for Phil Serrell as they travel from Dumfries and Galloway to Edinburgh, stopping to look for gold in the highest village in the country(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal talks to the couple who managed to avoid elimination at the weekend, and takes a closer look at the performances. Plus, interviews with the remaining dancers(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Matt Baker and Michelle Ackerley present(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Stuart is secretly pleased that Ben and Callum's date did not go to plan, although his mood switches when the Highway brothers receives a surprise visit from dad Jonno. Bex continues to struggle at the thought of leaving Walford and reminisces about Shakil. Jack feels out of his depth at his first day back with the police, Denise's obsession with Sheree and Patrick proves to be the final straw and Jean tries to keep Daniel's spirits up(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Chloe is interviewed over her assault, while Ange battles to keep Evan alive, but with so many conflicting versions of events, it remains uncertain if the truth will out. Cameron is furious, and determined to go to any lengths to see justice is done. Ric tries to support his granddaughter while secretly arranging an MRI scan to uncover the cause of his declining cognitive abilities(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: The truth behind what happened to Shaun is revealed to him as he finally comes face to face with the powers behind his downfall. Rachel seeks answers of her own after her suspicions that Shaun has been set up are confirmed by a vital piece of evidence. However, the truth they both uncover places their lives in great danger(n)

programme.title: Junior Doctors: On the Front Line
programme.desc: The doctors spend their first Christmas working in the hospital. Zohaib faces a difficult conversation, Nick deals with an awkward patient, and Howra has to certify her first death(n)

programme.title: Japan with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue Perkins continues on her trip around Japan, exploring a nation caught between the demands of a hi-tech future and the pull of a traditional past. In the concluding episode, Sue travels to the ancient city of Kyoto to spend time with geishas and meets those working hard to keep geisha culture alive. She then heads south of Kyoto to experience free diving with Ama divers, before travelling to Hiroshima, where she meets Mr Tetsushi, an 84-year-old survivor of the atomic blast during the Second World War. Finally, Sue returns to Tokyo, to see how the Japanese are tackling challenges of the future(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Consumer affairs show tackling issues around the UK. In a battle against suspect deals, the team digs into the facts and demands answers from people at the top, as well as showing viewers how to get the necessary information to make practical choices about their finances(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets a couple renovating a home in Dorset, Martel Maxwell visits a mid-terrace in Greater Manchester and Dion Dublin meets a first-time developer in Ellesmere Port(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: The work of police extradition units in the UK and Europe, following officers seeking out people fleeing from justice(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Sheriffs Tommy and Adie have an unusual call out to a sheep farm in Wales. Meanwhile, enforcement officers Aftab and John attempt to get money from a garage owed to a single mother(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is joined at an antiques fair at Wetherby Racecourse by experts Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt, who help two teams buy three items to take to auction in Darlington(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel notices that Becky is feeling smothered, and Valerie has a tough decision to make. Meanwhile, Emma spearheads a blood drive at The Campus(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple hunting for a home in Derbyshire on a budget of £750,000, and visits a long-standing family-run bootmakers in the Peak District(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell head to an auction in Hamilton. Steph comes across a chic French sign, but Phil is distracted by the local cuisine(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball presents more backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing. Plus, Ian Waite shares his thoughts on the couples' training progress in Waite's Warm Up(n)

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Welsh Conservative Party(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The six remaining celebrity contestants take part in the semi-finals, and are challenged to recreate a dish by the world-renowned chef and food writer, Yotam Ottolenghi. For the second task, the celebrities place in the competition rests on a dish inspired by someone they love or admire(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents further coverage of day six of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar, featuring the women's 200m and men's 110m hurdles. Dina Asher-Smith entered the 200m as one of the favourites to prevail in a discipline where there had not been a British medallist since Kathy Smallwood-Cook took bronze in the inaugural 1983 event in Helsinki. With analysis from Michael Johnson(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The business-based reality show returns. A new group of 16 hopeful candidates embark on their first challenge of the competition. The contestants are sent 6,000 miles from London to the capital city of Cape Town in South Africa, where they are tasked to set up and run their own safari and vineyard tours. One team's brash approach to ticket sales causes commotion, while on the other team premium prices prove problematic(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad and Bethan Rhys Roberts present a weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: Adam Frisby and the In The Style team get to work on a collection for 21-year-old Lottie Tomlinson, a social media influencer with over three million followers. Hoping to impress her, Adam decides to send her on a trip to Ibiza to shoot the campaign. While Lottie is ultimately happy with the items, Adam is concerned the designs are very niche and may not prove as popular as their previous collection(n)

programme.title: Stupid Man, Smart Phone
programme.desc: Russell Kane travels through Norway, heading into the Arctic Circle in the hope of seeing the Northern Lights. He is accompanied on his journey by YouTube stars Rose and Rosie, and together they rely on a smartphone to provide answers to all the challenges the expedition throws their way(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels through the Borders, learning handling techniques at a school for sheepdogs and then heading to some man-made lochs in the Moorfoot Hills. In Edinburgh, he goes in search of a lost river and two long-vanished lochs in the heart of the city, before walking in the footsteps of Mary, Queen of Scots on a allegedly haunted island in Loch Leven(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: Adam Frisby and the In The Style team get to work on a collection for 21-year-old Lottie Tomlinson, a social media influencer with over three million followers. Hoping to impress her, Adam decides to send her on a trip to Ibiza to shoot the campaign. While Lottie is ultimately happy with the items, Adam is concerned the designs are very niche and may not prove as popular as their previous collection(n)

programme.title: Stupid Man, Smart Phone
programme.desc: Russell Kane travels through Norway, heading into the Arctic Circle in the hope of seeing the Northern Lights. He is accompanied on his journey by YouTube stars Rose and Rosie, and together they rely on a smartphone to provide answers to all the challenges the expedition throws their way(n)

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin explore a two-bed terrace in Salford, a three-bed semi in Derbyshire and a one-bedroom flat in south London(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Following the investigation by an elite team of Scotland Yard detectives into the biggest organised crime gangs in London, which uncovered the drugs kingpin behind a multimillion-pound racket. By making his own drugs delivery, Paul Monk blew his cover and, when his associates were arrested, fled abroad(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Rob and Gerald brave the red carpet at a celebrity hotspot in London to try to recover the unpaid wages of a former worker, and there is an angry confrontation in a motorcycle shop when Mike and Lawrence try to remove bikes to collect a debt owed to a customer(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and Nick Hall help two teams look for items in the West Sussex town of Ardingly, while presenter Anita Manning investigates the history of funfairs(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Eve is a little more optimistic about the future and Valerie faces a tragedy, while Rob sustains an injury at work, and one of his colleagues acts strangely around the hospital(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in rural Somerset with two sport enthusiasts who have a £1million budget and want to buy a country home with views and a garden for football(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell go in search for items in Cumbria, setting out from Cockermouth and heading towards an auction near Kendal(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by three of the couples, including the first to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Matt Baker and Michelle Ackerley present(n)

programme.title: Parents' Evening
programme.desc: The return of the documentary examining the meetings between parents and teachers, beginning with one for Year 14 at Oakgrove Integrated College in Londonderry. The programme finds out how Jack, Aliah and Aidan are coping with their final year of A-levels and if their grades will be good enough to get into university(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben agrees to go on a date with Callum, although he begins to wonder if it is such a good idea after both Ian and Jay comment on his history with men - and things only get worse when Stuart pleads with him not to hurt his brother. Bex is fed up of Sonia fussing over university so by way of distraction she organises a baby shower for Louise. Mel continues to pressurise Sharon into getting her a villa in Portugal, Denise confronts Wanda over her treatment of Ted and Jean supports Daniel at his hospital appointments(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A year on from the assassination of Jamal Khashoggi, Jane Corbin investigates the brutal murder of the journalist inside the Saudi consulate in Istanbul. She speaks to some of the very few people who have listened to covert recordings from inside the diplomatic building, revealing the full details of what happened to Khashoggi, the hit squad sent to kill him and the cover-up that followed. She also hears evidence that responsibility for the murder goes to the highest levels of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and asks whether the West has turned a blind eye to state-sanctioned murder so that lucrative trade deals can flourish(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The eight remaining contestants take part in the first of the semi-finals and face their toughest challenge yet as they battle gale force winds as they cook in tents perched high on top of the White Cliffs of Dover. They need to defy the elements to serve up a feast for 120 HM Coastguard staff and volunteers responsible for keeping one of the world's busiest shipping lanes safe. They then head back to the MasterChef kitchen for an invention test, before two more celebrities are sent home(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: The story of a man who abandoned a financial career to pursue a childhood ambition to become a professional rabbit-catcher. He rejects modern pest control methods such as gas or poisons as environmentally dangerous, instead favouring the traditional skills he learned during his childhood in Magilligan, which involve using ferrets and dogs to herd animals towards him, so he can humanely kill them(n)

programme.title: Pop Goes Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Music and news from 1975, the year in which the IRA declared an indefinite ceasefire and secret talks took place between the British government and Sinn Fein. William Craig presented a compromise of voluntary power-sharing that was hoped would form a solution to Northern Ireland's divisions - but it was swiftly rejected by Unionists(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a documentary following young people from two local estates in London's Waltham Forest as they take part in a ground-breaking arts intervention programme designed to change the course of their lives for good. With help from mentors including Young Vic artistic director Kwame Kwei-Armah, EastEnders star Tameka Empson and composer Talvin Singh, the cast write and perform a musical based on their lives, as part of the capital's first Borough of Culture celebrations(n)

programme.title: The Cameron Years
programme.desc: The second of the two-part documentary begins in 2005 at a moment of crisis for the Conservative Party, following three successive general election defeats. David Cameron, a relatively new MP, and his small group of supporters sought to radically shift the party's direction, taking on veteran opponent David Davis in a leadership election. Here, Cameron re-examines his desire to reshape the party - and how he risked alienating the powerful old guard as he set out to prioritise issues such as environmentalism, social justice and international aid(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela Rippon test drives an electric car and discovers if the network of charging points needed is enough to support the vehicles. Plus, the dangers of online banking(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin check out a chalet bungalow in Beccles, Norfolk, and projects in Cwmaman in Wales and Sneyd Green, near Stoke(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: The cases of a lodger who killed his landlord and fled to Ireland, a homeless man with two years to serve on a prison sentence, and a Polish drink driver on the run in London(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Two sheriffs are forced to call the police when they are confronted by a gang of threatening men, while another is in for a surprise during a call to a debtor's house(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from London's Portobello Road, as experts Natasha Raskin and Thomas Plant support the red and blue teams scouring the famous street market. Catherine Southon acts as auctioneer(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie's tragic news divides opinion at The Mill, Zara tries to hide her hatred of Becky, and Rob faces outrage from a victim support group(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a garlic specialist who wants to establish a new business to find a new home in Shropshire on a £550 000 budget. Plus, a look at the expert craft of harp-making(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steph Connell has a birthday surprise for Phil Serrell as they travel from Dumfries and Galloway to Edinburgh, stopping to look for gold in the highest village in the country(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal talks to the couple who managed to avoid elimination at the weekend, and takes a closer look at the performances. Plus, interviews with the remaining dancers(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Matt Baker and Michelle Ackerley present(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Stuart is secretly pleased that Ben and Callum's date did not go to plan, although his mood switches when the Highway brothers receives a surprise visit from dad Jonno. Bex continues to struggle at the thought of leaving Walford and reminisces about Shakil. Jack feels out of his depth at his first day back with the police, Denise's obsession with Sheree and Patrick proves to be the final straw and Jean tries to keep Daniel's spirits up(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Chloe is interviewed over her assault, while Ange battles to keep Evan alive, but with so many conflicting versions of events, it remains uncertain if the truth will out. Cameron is furious, and determined to go to any lengths to see justice is done. Ric tries to support his granddaughter while secretly arranging an MRI scan to uncover the cause of his declining cognitive abilities(n)

programme.title: The Troubles: A Secret History
programme.desc: Jennifer O'Leary examines how British intelligence used informers to penetrate the IRA, revealing the one flaw in the organisation's supposedly impenetrable security that was used to unlock its secrets. The strategy culminated in the Loughgall ambush, a face-off between the SAS and the IRA that left eight members of the paramilitary group dead(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: The truth behind what happened to Shaun is revealed to him as he finally comes face to face with the powers behind his downfall. Rachel seeks answers of her own after her suspicions that Shaun has been set up are confirmed by a vital piece of evidence. However, the truth they both uncover places their lives in great danger(n)

programme.title: Junior Doctors: On the Front Line
programme.desc: The doctors spend their first Christmas working in the hospital. Zohaib faces a difficult conversation, Nick deals with an awkward patient, and Howra has to certify her first death(n)

programme.title: Japan with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue Perkins continues on her trip around Japan, exploring a nation caught between the demands of a hi-tech future and the pull of a traditional past. In the concluding episode, Sue travels to the ancient city of Kyoto to spend time with geishas and meets those working hard to keep geisha culture alive. She then heads south of Kyoto to experience free diving with Ama divers, before travelling to Hiroshima, where she meets Mr Tetsushi, an 84-year-old survivor of the atomic blast during the Second World War. Finally, Sue returns to Tokyo, to see how the Japanese are tackling challenges of the future(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A year on from the assassination of Jamal Khashoggi, Jane Corbin investigates the brutal murder of the journalist inside the Saudi consulate in Istanbul. She speaks to some of the very few people who have listened to covert recordings from inside the diplomatic building, revealing the full details of what happened to Khashoggi, the hit squad sent to kill him and the cover-up that followed. She also hears evidence that responsibility for the murder goes to the highest levels of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and asks whether the West has turned a blind eye to state-sanctioned murder so that lucrative trade deals can flourish(n)

programme.desc: A biting, cold winter delays exterior work on the puffer so Duncan keeps himself occupied by taking on the restoration of a vintage Ford truck. programme.title: Call air Cladach Ile (The Loss on Islay's Shore)
programme.desc: The story of two sea tragedies that brought the First World War to the Hebrides. In February 1918, the troopship Tuscania was torpedoed by a German U-boat. More than 200 American soldiers and British crew died as lifeboats were dashed against Islay's cliffs. Eight months later, HMS Otranto collided with another ship in its convoy in a fierce storm, killing nearly 500 men. Just 19 reached Islay's shore alive(n)

programme.title: 999: Britain From Above
programme.desc: Part one of two. As crime rises and budgets are slashed, police are taking to the air to keep one step ahead of the criminals. With access to the National Police Air Service's operation centre and state-of-the-art helicopters equipped with the latest technology, this programme joins crews as they are dispatched to the country's most critical incidents, from high-speed car chases to catching criminals in the dead of night. Paul Bown presents(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leaving Kel unconscious in the ginnel, Paul hurries away, Fiz is stunned to find Jade pouring her heart out to Evelyn about her abusive ex husband, and tired of Adam's obsession with Gary, Sarah heads out in a huff(n)

programme.title: A Confession
programme.desc: Steve Fulcher is lauded by the media for leading an investigation that recovered the bodies of two women murdered by Christopher Halliwell, but behind the scenes faces a disciplinary hearing at the hands of the IPCC, charged with gross misconduct. Karen Edwards refuses to give up her fight to get justice for her daughter Becky, and goes to meet with her local MP Robert Buckland to discuss a change that she thinks needs to made to the Police and Criminal Evidence Act. Starring Martin Freeman and Imelda Staunton(n)

programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: Documentary going inside the walls of six prisons to offer a sharp insight into the harsh realities of life behind bars. Thirty-year-old repeat offender Gemma, serving five years for burglary at HMP Downview in Surrey, talks about how her upbringing led her into a life of crime. She has a reputation for bad behaviour - which soon becomes apparent when she threatens a fellow prisoner. Meanwhile, new officer Jon Aldridge begins his first shift at HMP Bullingdon in Oxfordshire and seeks advice from one of the most respected men on the wing - top dog Anthony(n)

However, her birth mother refused to sign the necessary legal papers, meaning she was not officially adopted by her new parents. Over time, the truth was gradually revealed, but all communication was severed when Sharon moved to Germany. In one of their most complex cases yet, Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell try to reunite the 50-year-old with her biological mum and uncover an extraordinary connection between Sharon's quest and a man on his own search for answers(n)

programme.desc: Part one of two. As crime rises and budgets are slashed, police are taking to the air to keep one step ahead of the criminals. With access to the National Police Air Service's operation centre and state-of-the-art helicopters equipped with the latest technology, this programme joins crews as they are dispatched to the country's most critical incidents, from high-speed car chases to catching criminals in the dead of night. programme.title: A Confession
programme.desc: Steve Fulcher is lauded by the media for leading an investigation that recovered the bodies of two women murdered by Christopher Halliwell, but behind the scenes faces a disciplinary hearing at the hands of the IPCC, charged with gross misconduct. Karen Edwards refuses to give up her fight to get justice for her daughter Becky, and goes to meet with her local MP Robert Buckland to discuss a change that she thinks needs to made to the Police and Criminal Evidence Act. Starring Martin Freeman and Imelda Staunton(n)

programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: Documentary going inside the walls of six prisons to offer a sharp insight into the harsh realities of life behind bars. Thirty-year-old repeat offender Gemma, serving five years for burglary at HMP Downview in Surrey, talks about how her upbringing led her into a life of crime. She has a reputation for bad behaviour - which soon becomes apparent when she threatens a fellow prisoner. Meanwhile, new officer Jon Aldridge begins his first shift at HMP Bullingdon in Oxfordshire and seeks advice from one of the most respected men on the wing - top dog Anthony(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Minutes after she was born, Sharon Temple-Sowerby was given to a couple who were desperate for a child. However, her birth mother refused to sign the necessary legal papers, meaning she was not officially adopted by her new parents. Over time, the truth was gradually revealed, but all communication was severed when Sharon moved to Germany. In one of their most complex cases yet, Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell try to reunite the 50-year-old with her biological mum and uncover an extraordinary connection between Sharon's quest and a man on his own search for answers(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits a new age commune, where a man has fallen to his death, or may have been pushed. The dead man turns out to have a connection to the leader's shady past - they set the commune up together as a confidence trick, but the leader came to believe in what he preaches. A further death raises greater suspicion, especially since a vast inheritance might be coming the group's way. Guest starring Stephen Moyer (True Blood)(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bellamy falls in love with the victim of a handbag snatch, and the unlikely pair become the talk of the village as they go out on a series of dates. Merton attempts to track down porcelain figures stolen during a crime spree in nearby Whitby - and is given a new lead when a similar theft occurs at Mr Osbourne's house(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the murder of a wealthy man whose double life left him vulnerable to exploitation. Away from work, Rachel's recent moment of indiscretion threatens to come back to haunt her when a confused Sean takes a phone call from her one-night stand, and after Ade is pushed into living with his girlfriend on a permanent basis, Janet asks her mother to move in(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Troubled saleswoman Helen (Nicola Walker), whose mother was murdered back in episode one, turns up on Janet's doorstep with information that takes the investigation in a completely different direction - paving the way for the formidable Det Supt Julie Dodson to be called in. Meanwhile, new recruit Sgt Rob Waddington arrives on the scene - putting Janet's nose out of joint as she is demoted back down to constable. Emmerdale star Danny Miller (Aaron Livesy) joins the cast(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Pawnbroker Peter Lawson is shot dead in an armed robbery at his premises and Frost's investigations unearth a tragic family secret. He is convinced Lawson was killed by one of his children in revenge for years of systematic sexual abuse - but the truth turns out to be even more chilling. David Jason stars, with Diane Keen, Isla Blair, Matt Bardock, Anthony Bate, Mark Lambert and Edward Purver(n)
